instructions: Generate a Next.js login page at pages/login.tsx using TypeScript and the Pages Router
Import NextPage from 'next' and annotate the component as NextPage<ILoginPageProps>
The page does NOT use getStaticProps — it has no server-side data dependencies
Use controlled inputs managed via React.useState for email, password, errors, and isSubmitting
Validate on blur and on submit — do NOT validate on every keystroke
Clear the error for a field when the user types in it (onChange handler)
Email validation: non-empty check first, then regex format check
Password validation: non-empty check first, then minimum 8 characters check
Show inline error messages directly beneath the respective input field
Disable the submit button while isSubmitting is true
Accept an optional onSubmit prop of type (credentials: { email: string; password: string }) => void for testability
Use CSS Modules for all styles — import from '../styles/Login.module.css'
Center the form card on the viewport; max-width 400px on desktop, 100% on mobile
Write unit tests in __tests__/pages/login.test.tsx covering all 7 scenarios listed in the spec
Mock next/router at the top of the test file
Use userEvent from @testing-library/user-event for all interaction tests
